issues.title: POAM-011: CRITICAL AGING — OLA Audit Finding Open 2+ Years (AC-6 Least Privilege)
issues.description:
**POAM ID:** POAM-2024-019 (Original)
**Source:** External Audit - OLA Annual Review 2024
**Framework:** State Security Policy / NIST AC-6
**Control:** AC-6 Least Privilege
**Severity:** High → Escalated to Critical (aging)
**Discovery Date:** 2024-03-15
**Original Due Date:** 2024-09-15
**Current Age:** 810 days — ⛔ 2+ YEARS OPEN
**Assigned Team:** Originally ISSO Team
**Assigned Owner:** Unassigned (ownership gap)

### What Happened

OLA annual audit identified excessive admin privileges across licensing portal. Finding was shared with ISSO team via email. No formal tracking was established.

### Root Cause of Delay

1. Finding shared via email only — never entered into tracking system
2. ISSO team assumed application team owned remediation
3. Application team was never notified
4. No follow-up cadence established
5. Finding resurfaced during 2026 OLA assessment prep

### Impact

* 2+ years of unnecessary admin access across 47 user accounts
* Repeat finding expected in upcoming OLA audit
* Potential compliance action from state auditor
* Loss of credibility with OLA assessors

### Corrective Actions (NOW IN TRACKING)

1. ⏳ Immediate privilege review and reduction (Target: June 10)
2. ⬜ Implement quarterly access reviews for OLA portal
3. ⬜ Establish RACI matrix for all external audit findings
4. ⬜ Configure automated alerts for aging POA&Ms
5. ⬜ Brief leadership on accountability gap

### Lessons Learned

This finding is the catalyst for the new GRC Trust Center accountability workflow. All external findings now require:

* Formal intake into tracking system within 24 hours
* Named owner with acceptance confirmation
* Automated escalation at 30/60/90 day marks
* Monthly leadership visibility on aging items

**Assigned Team:** Application Security
**Assigned Owner:** Carlos Reyes
**Days Open:** 5

### Finding Detail

Blind SQL injection identified in Medicaid eligibility lookup API endpoint. Parameterized queries not enforced on 2 of 14 API endpoints.

### Affected Endpoints

* /api/v2/eligibility/lookup (CONFIRMED)
* /api/v2/eligibility/batch-verify (CONFIRMED)

### Remediation Plan

1. ✅ WAF rule deployed as interim mitigation (May 29)
2. ⏳ Refactor to parameterized queries
3. ⬜ SAST scan of all API endpoints
4. ⬜ Retest by Bishop Fox
5. ⬜ Submit closure evidence to CMS

### Escalation

CMS notification required if not remediated within 30 days per ARC-AMPE.

issues.description:

**Source:** IRS Safeguard Review
**Framework:** IRS Publication 1075, Section 7.3
**Control:** SC-28 Protection of Information at Rest
**Severity:** Critical
**Discovery Date:** 2026-03-15
**Due Date:** 2026-06-15 ⚠️ APPROACHING
**Assigned Team:** Database Administration
**Assigned Owner:** Maria Lopez (DBA Lead)

### Finding

Federal Tax Information (FTI) stored in legacy Oracle database lacks FIPS 140-2 validated encryption. IRS Pub 1075 requires all FTI at rest to be encrypted using FIPS 140-2 validated modules.

### Remediation Plan

1. Procure Oracle TDE with FIPS 140-2 validated module
2. Test encryption in staging environment
3. Schedule maintenance window for production migration
4. Validate with FIPS 140-2 certificate documentation
5. Submit evidence to IRS Office of Safeguards

### Impact if Not Remediated

IRS may restrict or revoke FTI access, impacting Unemployment Insurance and Tax processing programs.

issues.title: POAM-011: CRITICAL AGING — OLA Audit Finding Open 2+ Years (AC-6 Least Privilege)
issues.description:
**POAM ID:** POAM-2024-019 (Original)
**Source:** External Audit - OLA Annual Review 2024
**Framework:** State Security Policy / NIST AC-6
**Control:** AC-6 Least Privilege
**Severity:** High → Escalated to Critical (aging)
**Discovery Date:** 2024-03-15
**Original Due Date:** 2024-09-15
**Current Age:** 810 days — ⛔ 2+ YEARS OPEN
**Assigned Team:** Originally ISSO Team
**Assigned Owner:** Unassigned (ownership gap)

### What Happened

OLA annual audit identified excessive admin privileges across licensing portal. Finding was shared with ISSO team via email. No formal tracking was established.

### Root Cause of Delay

1. Finding shared via email only — never entered into tracking system
2. ISSO team assumed application team owned remediation
3. Application team was never notified
4. No follow-up cadence established
5. Finding resurfaced during 2026 OLA assessment prep

### Impact

* 2+ years of unnecessary admin access across 47 user accounts
* Repeat finding expected in upcoming OLA audit
* Potential compliance action from state auditor
* Loss of credibility with OLA assessors

### Corrective Actions (NOW IN TRACKING)

1. ⏳ Immediate privilege review and reduction (Target: June 10)
2. ⬜ Implement quarterly access reviews for OLA portal
3. ⬜ Establish RACI matrix for all external audit findings
4. ⬜ Configure automated alerts for aging POA&Ms
5. ⬜ Brief leadership on accountability gap

### Lessons Learned

This finding is the catalyst for the new GRC Trust Center accountability workflow. All external findings now require:

* Formal intake into tracking system within 24 hours
* Named owner with acceptance confirmation
* Automated escalation at 30/60/90 day marks
* Monthly leadership visibility on aging items

**Severity:** Medium
**Status:** Monitoring - Under Investigation

**Reported By:** DLP Alert (Automated)

issues.title: IR-002: Resolved - Unauthorized Access Attempt on DDS System
issues.description:
**Incident ID:** IR-2026-002
**Severity:** Medium
**Status:** Resolved - Post-Incident Review Complete
**Detected:** 2026-05-18 14:20 EST
**Resolved:** 2026-05-19 11:00 EST
**Duration:** 20 hours 40 minutes

### Summary

Multiple failed authentication attempts detected on DDS Case Management System from unauthorized IP range. Automated lockout triggered after 5 attempts.

### Notifications Sent

| Framework | Notified | Method |
| -- | -- | -- |
| SSA EIEP | 2026-05-18 15:15 | Phone + Email |
| State CISO | 2026-05-18 15:00 | Email |

### Root Cause

Former contractor credentials not disabled within 24-hour SLA. Credentials used from contractor's personal network.

### Corrective Actions

1. ✅ Credentials revoked immediately
2. ✅ SSA notified within 1-hour SLA
3. ✅ Contractor offboarding checklist updated
4. ✅ Automated account disable on termination date implemented
5. ✅ Post-incident review completed May 22

POAM-2026-016 created for contractor access management process improvement.

**Assigned Owner:** Rachel Adams (Security Engineering Lead)

issues.title: IR-001: Active Incident - Suspected Phishing Campaign Targeting Medicaid Staff
issues.description:
**Incident ID:** IR-2026-001
**Severity:** High
**Status:** Active - Containment Phase
**Detected:** 2026-06-01 08:45 EST
**Reported By:** SOC Analyst (Alex Johnson)
**Incident Commander:** Rachel Adams

### Timeline

| Time | Event |
| -- | -- |
| 08:45 | Phishing email detected by email gateway (3 recipients) |
| 08:52 | SOC analyst confirms malicious payload |
| 09:00 | Incident declared - IR plan activated |
| 09:15 | 2 of 3 recipients identified - accounts locked |
| 09:30 | 3rd recipient clicked link - workstation isolated |
| 10:00 | Forensic imaging of affected workstation initiated |
| 10:30 | CMS notified (1-hour SLA met) |

### Notification Status

| Framework | SLA | Notified | Status |
| -- | -- | -- | -- |
| CMS ARC-AMPE | 1 hour | 10:30 EST | ✅ Met |
| IRS Pub 1075 | 45 days (if FTI involved) | TBD | ⏳ Assessing |
| SSA EIEP | 1 hour (if SSA data) | N/A | ✅ Not applicable |
| State Law | 24 hours | Not yet | ⏳ Pending scope |

### Key Questions

* Did affected user have FTI access? → Under investigation
* Was any CMS data accessed from compromised workstation? → Forensics in progress
* Lateral movement detected? → No indicators so far

### Next Steps

1. Complete forensic analysis of workstation
2. Determine if FTI/CMS data was exposed
3. Issue IRS notification if FTI involved
4. Prepare incident report for leadership briefing

### Finding

issues.title: POAM-013: High - Pen Test Finding: Weak TLS Configuration on FTI Gateway
issues.description:
**POAM ID:** POAM-2026-013
**Source:** Penetration Test - Q2 2026
**Framework:** IRS Pub 1075 / NIST SC-8
**Control:** SC-8 Transmission Confidentiality
**Severity:** High
**Discovery Date:** 2026-05-28
**Due Date:** 2026-06-28
**Assigned Team:** Infrastructure
**Assigned Owner:** Tom Chen
**Days Open:** 5

### Finding

FTI gateway accepting TLS 1.0/1.1 connections. IRS Pub 1075 requires TLS 1.2+ for all FTI transmissions. FIPS 140-2 compliance at risk.

### Remediation

1. ⏳ Disable TLS 1.0/1.1 on FTI gateway
2. ⬜ Update cipher suite to FIPS-approved only
3. ⬜ Test connectivity with all FTI data partners
4. ⬜ Validate with Qualys SSL Labs scan

### Risk

### Finding Detail

Blind SQL injection identified in Medicaid eligibility lookup API endpoint. Parameterized queries not enforced on 2 of 14 API endpoints.

### Affected Endpoints

4. ⬜ Retest by Bishop Fox
5. ⬜ Submit closure evidence to CMS

### Escalation

CMS notification required if not remediated within 30 days per ARC-AMPE.

issues.title: POAM-011: CRITICAL AGING — OLA Audit Finding Open 2+ Years (AC-6 Least Privilege)
issues.description:
**POAM ID:** POAM-2024-019 (Original)
**Source:** External Audit - OLA Annual Review 2024
**Framework:** State Security Policy / NIST AC-6
**Control:** AC-6 Least Privilege
**Severity:** High → Escalated to Critical (aging)
**Discovery Date:** 2024-03-15
**Original Due Date:** 2024-09-15
**Current Age:** 810 days — ⛔ 2+ YEARS OPEN
**Assigned Team:** Originally ISSO Team
**Assigned Owner:** Unassigned (ownership gap)

### What Happened

OLA annual audit identified excessive admin privileges across licensing portal. Finding was shared with ISSO team via email. No formal tracking was established.

### Root Cause of Delay

1. Finding shared via email only — never entered into tracking system
2. ISSO team assumed application team owned remediation
3. Application team was never notified
4. No follow-up cadence established
5. Finding resurfaced during 2026 OLA assessment prep

### Impact

* 2+ years of unnecessary admin access across 47 user accounts
* Repeat finding expected in upcoming OLA audit
* Potential compliance action from state auditor
* Loss of credibility with OLA assessors

### Corrective Actions (NOW IN TRACKING)

1. ⏳ Immediate privilege review and reduction (Target: June 10)
2. ⬜ Implement quarterly access reviews for OLA portal
3. ⬜ Establish RACI matrix for all external audit findings
4. ⬜ Configure automated alerts for aging POA&Ms
5. ⬜ Brief leadership on accountability gap

### Lessons Learned

This finding is the catalyst for the new GRC Trust Center accountability workflow. All external findings now require:

* Formal intake into tracking system within 24 hours
* Named owner with acceptance confirmation
* Automated escalation at 30/60/90 day marks
* Monthly leadership visibility on aging items

issues.title: POAM-007: Medium - OLA Portal - Session Timeout Non-Compliant
issues.description:
**POAM ID:** POAM-2026-007
**Source:** OLA Security Assessment
**Framework:** State Security Policy / NIST AC-12
**Control:** AC-12 Session Termination
**Severity:** Medium
**Discovery Date:** 2026-05-15
**Due Date:** 2026-07-15
**Assigned Team:** Development
**Assigned Owner:** Carlos Reyes

### Finding

OLA Portal sessions remain active for 8 hours. State policy requires 30-minute idle timeout for systems processing PII. NIST AC-12 requires automatic session termination after defined period.

### Remediation Plan

1. Update session management configuration
2. Implement 30-minute idle timeout
3. Add session warning at 25 minutes
4. Test with QA team
5. Deploy in next sprint

### Estimated Level of Effort

2 story points. Sprint 14 (June 8-22).

issues.title: POAM-006: High - CMS Continuous Monitoring Reports Overdue
issues.description:
**POAM ID:** POAM-2026-006
**Source:** CMS Monitoring Requirement
**Framework:** CMS ARC-AMPE / NIST CA-7
**Control:** CA-7 Continuous Monitoring
**Severity:** High
**Discovery Date:** 2026-05-01
**Due Date:** 2026-06-01 ⚠️ OVERDUE
**Assigned Team:** Compliance
**Assigned Owner:** Mike Torres

### Finding

Monthly Continuous Monitoring (ConMon) reports to CMS are 2 months behind. CMS requires monthly submission of:

* Vulnerability scan results
* POA&M updates
* Significant change notifications
* Incident reports

### Overdue Reports

* March 2026 ConMon: Submitted (late)
* April 2026 ConMon: Draft complete, pending review
* May 2026 ConMon: Not started

### Root Cause

Compliance analyst resignation in March. Position backfilled May 15.

### Remediation

1. Submit April report by June 5
2. Submit May report by June 15
3. Establish backup ConMon responsibility
4. Automate report generation where possible

issues.title: POAM-004: Critical - SSA PII Exposed via Misconfigured API
issues.description:
**POAM ID:** POAM-2026-004
**Source:** Penetration Test
**Framework:** SSA EIEP Requirements / NIST AC-3, SC-7
**Control:** AC-3 Access Enforcement
**Severity:** Critical
**Discovery Date:** 2026-05-20
**Due Date:** 2026-06-05
**Assigned Team:** Application Security
**Assigned Owner:** Jake Morrison

### Finding

DDS Case Management API endpoint returns SSA PII (SSN, disability determination data) without proper authorization checks. API gateway misconfiguration allows unauthenticated queries.

### Immediate Actions Taken

1. ✅ API endpoint disabled (May 20, 2026 - 2 hours after discovery)
2. ✅ SSA notified per 1-hour breach notification requirement
3. ✅ Forensic analysis: no evidence of unauthorized access
4. ⏳ Fix authorization middleware
5. ⬜ Security code review of all SSA-connected APIs
6. ⬜ Re-enable with proper auth + pen test validation

### SSA Notification

SSA OIG notified 2026-05-20 at 14:32 EST (within 1-hour SLA). Case # SSA-INC-2026-0847.

issues.title: POAM-003: High - Audit Log Review Not Meeting NIST AU-6 Requirements
issues.description:
**POAM ID:** POAM-2026-003
**Source:** NIST Annual Assessment
**Framework:** NIST 800-53 Rev 5
**Control:** AU-6 Audit Review, Analysis, and Reporting
**Severity:** High
**Discovery Date:** 2026-02-10
**Due Date:** 2026-05-30 ⚠️ OVERDUE
**Assigned Team:** Security Operations
**Assigned Owner:** Alex Johnson (SOC Lead)

### Finding

Weekly audit log reviews not documented for 3 of 7 state systems. NIST AU-6 requires regular review of audit records.

### Affected Systems

* ❌ Unemployment Insurance Tax System
* ❌ Licensing Portal (OLA)
* ❌ Disability Determination System
* ✅ Medicaid Eligibility System
* ✅ Health Insurance Marketplace
* ✅ State Data Center Infrastructure
* ✅ Employee HR System

### Remediation

1. Configure automated log forwarding to Splunk for all 3 systems
2. Create weekly review SOPs
3. Assign log review responsibilities
4. Document review evidence in GRC tool

### Blocker

Splunk license expansion needed. Procurement approved, vendor provisioning ETA June 10.

issues.title: POAM-002: High - MFA Not Enforced for CMS Data Access (ARC-AMPE)
issues.description:
**POAM ID:** POAM-2026-002
**Source:** CMS ARC-AMPE Assessment
**Framework:** CMS ARC-AMPE / MARS-E 2.2
**Control:** IA-2(1) Multi-Factor Authentication
**Severity:** High
**Discovery Date:** 2026-04-01
**Due Date:** 2026-07-01
**Assigned Team:** Identity & Access Management
**Assigned Owner:** Priya Sharma

### Finding

CMS Hub connectivity users accessing Federal Data Services Hub (FDSH) data lack MFA enforcement. CMS MARS-E requires MFA for all users accessing CMS data.

### Remediation Plan

1. ✅ Deploy Okta MFA for all FDSH-connected accounts
2. ✅ Configure MFA policies (phishing-resistant preferred)
3. ⏳ Enroll remaining 45 users (of 230 total)
4. ⬜ Update ISA with CMS to reflect MFA implementation
5. ⬜ Submit POA&M closure evidence to CMS

### Progress: 80% complete

issues.title: POAM-001: Critical - FTI Database Encryption Non-Compliant (IRS Pub 1075)
issues.description:
**POAM ID:** POAM-2026-001
**Source:** IRS Safeguard Review
**Framework:** IRS Publication 1075, Section 7.3
**Control:** SC-28 Protection of Information at Rest
**Severity:** Critical
**Discovery Date:** 2026-03-15
**Due Date:** 2026-06-15 ⚠️ APPROACHING
**Assigned Team:** Database Administration
**Assigned Owner:** Maria Lopez (DBA Lead)

### Finding

Federal Tax Information (FTI) stored in legacy Oracle database lacks FIPS 140-2 validated encryption. IRS Pub 1075 requires all FTI at rest to be encrypted using FIPS 140-2 validated modules.

### Remediation Plan

1. Procure Oracle TDE with FIPS 140-2 validated module
2. Test encryption in staging environment
3. Schedule maintenance window for production migration
4. Validate with FIPS 140-2 certificate documentation
5. Submit evidence to IRS Office of Safeguards

### Impact if Not Remediated

IRS may restrict or revoke FTI access, impacting Unemployment Insurance and Tax processing programs.

issues.title: CMS ARC-AMPE Assessment - Medicaid Eligibility System
issues.description:

**Framework:** CMS ARC-AMPE / NIST 800-53 Rev 5
**System:** State Medicaid Eligibility & Enrollment System (MEES)
**Assessor:** CMS CISO Division
**State Owner:** Maria Gonzalez (State ISSO)
**Start Date:** 2026-05-01
**End Date:** 2026-07-31

### ARC-AMPE Requirements

* Minimum Acceptable Risk Standards for Exchanges (MARS-E 2.2)
* CMS Information Security Acceptable Risk Safeguards
* Annual security assessment per CMS TRA requirements

### Status

Evidence collection phase. 38 of 72 control evidence packages submitted to CMS.

### Key Findings So Far

* 2 Critical: IA-2 MFA gaps on admin accounts, SI-2 patching SLA breaches
* 4 High: AC-2 service account management, AU-6 log review gaps, CM-6 baseline drift, SC-28 encryption gaps
* 8 Medium findings across AC, AU, CM families
